evals.prompt: I have 3 independent API endpoints to test and also need a code review of the shared middleware. How should I structure this?
evals.expected_output: The agent recommends parallel execution for the 3 independent API tests, and either parallel with the middleware review or sequential if the middleware affects the tests. References the coordination strategies (Parallel, Sequential, Swarm, Hybrid).
assertions: The agent identifies the tasks as having some independence
The agent recommends a coordination strategy (parallel or hybrid)
The agent mentions quality gates between phases

evals.prompt: I need to implement a new feature, write tests for it, and then do a security review. The feature depends on an existing auth module. What's the best approach?
evals.expected_output: The agent recommends sequential execution: implement feature (depending on auth module) → write tests → security review. May suggest parallel for independent test writing.
assertions: The agent identifies the dependency on the auth module
The agent recommends sequential or hybrid strategy
The agent maps tasks to appropriate agents (feature-implementer, test-runner, security-auditor)

evals.prompt: My codebase has 5 separate modules that each need linter fixes. How do I speed this up?
evals.expected_output: The agent recommends parallel execution with 5 homogeneous agents (same type, different inputs) for maximum speedup. References the speedup formula.
assertions: The agent identifies the tasks as independent
The agent mentions using multiple agents of the same type

evals.prompt: We found a performance regression but aren't sure which component caused it. What coordination strategy should we use?
evals.expected_output: The agent recommends swarm coordination: multiple agents investigate different perspectives (profiler, code-reviewer for recent changes, benchmark runner) in parallel, then synthesize findings.
assertions: The agent recommends swarm or multi-perspective investigation
The agent suggests parallel investigation from different angles
The agent mentions synthesis of findings

evals.prompt: I need to refactor a legacy module. It has tests, but I want to make sure nothing breaks. How should I coordinate the work?
evals.expected_output: The agent recommends iterative or sequential coordination: run existing tests → refactor → re-run tests → code review. Quality gates between phases.
assertions: The agent includes test verification before and after refactoring
The agent assigns appropriate agents (refactorer, test-runner, code-reviewer)
